title: descurainia rydbergii
intro: Descurainia rydbergii, also known as Rydberg's wallflower or western bittercress, is a type of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The plant is native to the western United States and Canada, and its leaves, stems, and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Descurainia rydbergii include treating colds, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as supporting the immune system and reducing fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using its roots to create a tincture.
